questions.question: What is the primary role of an Instruction Set Architecture (ISA) in computer systems?
options: It defines the physical layout of transistors on a chip and their interconnections.
It specifies the set of instructions a processor can execute, its data types, registers, and memory model.
It primarily determines the clock frequency and power consumption characteristics of a Central Processing Unit (CPU).
It manages the operating system's interaction with peripheral devices and memory allocation.
It optimizes the compilation of high-level programming languages into machine-level code.

questions.question: In digital IC design, what is the primary purpose of 'Clock Gating'?
options: To prevent clock skew across different parts of the design by balancing clock path delays.
To reduce dynamic power consumption by disabling the clock signal to inactive functional blocks or flip-flops.
To synthesize a clock tree network with minimal delay and latency from the clock source to all registers.
To ensure that all sequential elements (flip-flops) sample their data on a specific edge of the clock signal.
To generate multiple derived clock frequencies from a single input clock for different circuit sections.

questions.question: In the context of chip design verification, what does 'functional coverage' primarily aim to measure?
options: The percentage of physical area utilized by the design on the silicon die.
The proportion of clock cycles during which the design operated without encountering any errors or assertions.
How thoroughly the design's specified features, behaviors, and corner cases have been exercised by the test suite.
The amount of memory used by the verification testbench and simulation environment.
The speed at which simulation tests can be executed and the overall simulation throughput.

questions.question: In modern multi-core processors, which cache write policy dictates that data is written simultaneously to both the cache and the main memory upon a write hit?
options: Write-back
Write-allocate
No-write-allocate
Write-through
Dirty bit policy

questions.question: In advanced semiconductor manufacturing, what phenomenon refers to the gradual transport of material, typically metal atoms, in an integrated circuit due to momentum transfer from conducting electrons, leading to potential open circuits or short circuits over time?
options: Hot Carrier Injection (HCI)
Negative Bias Temperature Instability (NBTI)
Electromigration
Dielectric Breakdown (DB)
Gate Oxide Leakage
